headline: Software Engineering · Backend Systems · Regulated-domain Operations · ML/AI
paragraphs: I am applying for the Backend Engineer -- Customer Identity role because it connects two parts of my background that are usually treated separately: backend software engineering and direct experience inside banking and insurance operations. Customer identity is not just another backend domain; it sits close to trust, onboarding, data quality, fraud exposure, user friction, and regulatory expectations.

My current engineering path is focused on backend systems, developer tooling, technical documentation, and ML/AI-adjacent workflows. I have worked with Java, Spring Boot, SQL, Python, Linux, Git, GitHub Actions, LaTeX tooling, and repository-backed validation processes. I care about software that is understandable, testable, documented, and traceable enough to survive real operational pressure.

Before moving fully into software engineering, I spent years in banking and insurance operations. That experience shaped how I reason about identity, verification, exceptions, auditability, handoffs, and the cost of small failures inside financial systems. It also makes N26's Customer Identity segment especially relevant to me: the engineering work has a direct relationship with user trust and institutional responsibility.

I would bring a backend-oriented profile with strong domain learning, regulated-context judgment, and the ability to connect technical implementation with operational consequences. I am comfortable working independently, but I value teams where knowledge is shared clearly and feedback improves the system rather than becoming ceremony.

This role matches the engineering path I want to build seriously: Java/Kotlin backend work in a product area where reliability, security, documentation, autonomy, and practical problem-solving are part of the same craft. I would be glad to discuss how my wider technical and regulated-domain background could be useful to the Customer Identity team.
public_note: Prepared for the N26 Backend Engineer -- Customer Identity application.
